1: First time in the Bastion? You'll get used to it. Before you head into a rift, here's a tip: if you Guard with a Glyph that knows an Interrupt technique, it'll fire off automatically when the conditions are right. It's the difference between a good Warden and a great one.
One more thing — don't forget about Recruit. Call out to a wild Glyph during battle and your capture odds go up. Three calls per fight, max. It's not glamorous, but it works.
2: You performed your first fusion? Good. Here's something they don't teach in the Academy: mixing affinities produces unexpected results. Don't be afraid to combine an Electric with a Ground. You might be surprised.
I've seen plenty of Wardens rely on raw power. The ones who survive? They learn to read the turn order. Speed controls the battlefield. A fast Glyph with Stun can shut down threats before they act.
3: The Major Rift is no joke. The boss in there — a Riftmaw — hits hard and fast. Phase 2 it switches to Piercing attacks, so your back row isn't safe. Bring something tanky with an Interrupt, or you'll be eating Null Beams all day.
Watch your squad composition. I've seen Wardens bring three damage dealers and wonder why they can't survive Phase 2. You need a healer, a tank, and a plan.
4: The Apex Rift... I won't sugarcoat it. The Nullweaver in there brings a full squad — Riftmaw, Vortail, the works. All Water-type. You'll want Ground advantage, but they'll try to debuff you into dust.
I've only seen two Wardens clear the Apex. One had a fully mastered team. The other was just stubborn. Be both.
5: Almost there, Warden. The deepest rifts are still waiting — don't let up now. The Major and Apex bosses are a different breed. Everything you've learned so far? You'll need it.
You've come further than most. But the final stretch is where good Wardens become legends. Tighten up your squad and finish this.
all_cleared: You've done what most Wardens only dream about. Every rift sealed, every boss bested. But the rifts don't stay quiet forever. Keep your team sharp — the echoes are getting stronger.
If you're looking for a challenge now, try re-entering the Apex with a team you haven't used before. The Nullweaver's squad changes tactics each time. There's always something new to learn.

1: The Codex is your most valuable tool. Every species you discover is catalogued there — and the ones you haven't found yet leave traces. Pay attention to the silhouettes. I've added notes where I can.
I've been tracking echo signatures in the rifts. Those ghostly encounters? They're not random. Each echo carries a memory fragment — a piece of this world's history before the rifts opened.
2: Fascinating — the Glyph you created doesn't match any species in my field records. I've updated the Codex. I wonder... there are several silhouettes that seem to resonate with storm energy. Perhaps try fusing two of the same affinity next?

The mastery system isn't just a training exercise. When a Glyph truly masters its techniques, its base capabilities improve permanently. Check the objectives in the detail popup — each one teaches you something about how that species fights best.
3: I've been studying Glyph #009 in the Codex — the one we haven't identified yet. Its energy signature resonates with both storm and fracture wavelengths. If I had to guess... you'd need to fuse an Electric Prime with a Water Prime to find it. But that's just a theory.
The Tier 3 species are remarkable. Each one represents the pinnacle of its affinity — Stormfang channels storms, Terradon reshapes stone, and Riftmaw... well, Riftmaw is something else entirely.
4: The Apex-tier species — Voltarion, Lithosurge, Nullweaver. They're not just powerful. My research suggests they existed before the rifts. The rifts didn't create them; they awakened them.

Your Codex completion is coming along. Every species you discover fills in another piece of the puzzle. I believe there's a pattern — the affinities aren't random. They're connected to something deeper.
5: We're so close to understanding the full picture. The deepest rifts hold species I've only theorized about. Keep exploring — every discovery brings us closer.
The data from your recent runs is extraordinary. But there are still gaps in the Codex. The answers lie in the rifts you haven't sealed yet.
all_cleared: You've discovered every species. The Codex is complete. But look at the memory fragments you've collected from echo encounters — they tell a story. The rifts aren't invading. They're remembering.
I'll keep studying the data. If you find anything unusual in re-entered rifts — echoes behaving differently, new patterns — bring it to me. Science never sleeps.

1: Your Crawler's seen better days, but she'll hold. Bring her back without a scratch sometime and I'll see what I can do about the hull plating.
Energy management is key in the rifts. Scanning costs 5 energy, but knowing what's ahead? Priceless. Don't burn it all on Reinforce early — save some for deeper floors.
2: Hey, you're filling up that Bench. If you find yourself turning down captures, come talk to me — I might be able to squeeze in another slot once you've done some exploring.
I've been tuning the Scan module. It'll reveal room types in a radius now, which means fewer surprises. Use it near clusters of unrevealed rooms for maximum coverage.
3: You've been through a lot of rifts. Your Crawler's got character now — battle scars and all. If you manage to clear the Major Rift, I've got something special waiting for your Capacity rating.
Repair items are rare but they can save a run. If your hull's getting low, don't be too proud to use one. A dead Crawler means a failed rift, and that's worse than burning a consumable.
4: The Apex Rift has 4 floors. Your energy economy needs to last the entire descent. I'd suggest being conservative with abilities on the first two floors and saving your reserves for the deep.
I've reinforced the hull for the Apex run, but the hazard damage in there is no joke. Watch those hazard rooms — they'll chip away at you faster than you'd expect.
5: You're in the home stretch now. The Crawler's holding up, but those last rifts will push her limits. Stock up on Hull Shields and Repair Patches before you dive in.
The deep rifts have nastier hazards. Four floors of punishment. Make sure your energy lasts — running dry down there means no scanning, no reinforcing, nothing.
all_cleared: Well, you did it. Every rift sealed. Your Crawler's been to places I never thought she'd go. I'm proud of both of you.
The Crawler's fully upgraded now. If you're heading back into rifts for captures, remember — the items you find can make or break a deep floor. Keep that inventory stocked.
